subject: Add Nexus Redundancy
commit_message:
Add Nexus Redundancy

Add nexus redundancy, which is audited by repo audit. Adds an
alternate nexus repo to be used in case the main repo goes down.

Issue-ID: POLICY-431
Change-Id: I25bcde39a93a1bfea7bd1535540c8dbf5cdc8c86
Signed-off-by: Temoc Rodriguez <cr056n@att.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-drools-pdp-master-verify-java/293/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/policy-drools-pdp-master-verify-java/293

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

The issue is that qa default ONAP deployment will not have a secondary nexus repo, but the settings.xml used will have an invalid entry after installation in the settings.xml.

I'm open for discussion but I tend to think that the right way to handle this functionality is to attach an "install" script to the appropriate feature, I think it may be the active-standby feature.

There are some minor changes in the base.conf associated with that but we can discuss offline if there's agreement.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Rather than making two repositories a requirement, it would be best to provide it as an option. Note that this means that there would now be a third 'settings.xml' variant to choose from (there is already a second 'standalone-settings.xml' file), and an alternate 'base.conf' to choose from.

I have been thinking of a generic way to make this kind of selection. Suppose there were a directory called 'settings.xml.select' which had three files in it: '01_twoRepositories', '02_singleRepository', '03_standAlone', which contains the three variants. The '${{VARNAME}}' variables are expanded in each of the files, and the one actually selected would be the first one that doesn't have any unexpanded '${{VARNAME}}' variables.

Note that this could be a generic mechanism -- any directory of the form 'xyzzy.select' could contain multiple candidates for the file 'xyzzy'. This would also allow a more generic 'configure' function in 'docker-install.sh', and eliminate the specialized 'configure_settings' function.

Patch Set 1:

OK, there is agreement then to take this back to design phase.   With regards on how it's done need more details on the proposals.   One important decision is if this is a base only feature (all the work in installation), or specifically associated with a feature (work on feature install hooks), or both.

Patch Set 1:

My understanding from Jorge is that this will be abandoned.
